title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026, part of the Sail250 Tall Ships tour, will occur from July 11-16 to celebrate the U.S. 250th anniversary.
Queen Elizabeth II's 1976 visit to Boston for the first Sail Boston marked a historic British royal presence in the city.
Sail Boston events faced protests in 1992 and 2000, with critics highlighting the tall ships' connection to the slave trade.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to Patriots rookie minicamp, bringing energy similar to last season despite recent off-field issues.
Mike Giardi reports improved atmosphere in Foxborough amid Patriots' preparations for the 2026 NFL season.
Patriots aim to bolster roster, potentially acquiring wide receiver A.J Brown, as they prepare for a challenging season.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive 426-foot two-run homer in the eighth inning to secure a 3-2 Red Sox win.
Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle achieved a career-high by pitching eight innings, giving up two runs and four hits.
Closer Aroldis Chapman recorded the save despite a bases-loaded ninth, ensuring Boston's comeback victory.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox place Trevor Story on 10-day injured list with sports hernia; Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester.
Trevor Story's potential surgery for sports hernia unresolved; recovery could be 6-10 weeks if surgery occurs.
Story, 33, struggling at .206 batting average and .547 OPS, has six errors in 39 games at shortstop this season.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man died after his pickup truck collided with a school bus in Marlborough, New Hampshire.
The crash involved a school bus carrying 13 students; no serious injuries were reported among those on board.
The incident is under investigation by New Hampshire State Police, who are examining the crash involving Route 12.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th inning led the Atlanta Braves to a 3-2 win over the Red Sox.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs and struck out six in five innings against the Braves.
The Red Sox tied the game with home runs from Mickey Gasper and Marcelo Mayer but couldn't score further.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs open to rejoining Patriots, but A.J. Brown trade complicates potential reunion, reports Albert Breer.
Diggs had a 1,000-yard receiving season with the Patriots but may not accept a third receiver role.
Patriots face cap constraints with Brown's $32 million AAV and Romeo Doubs' $68 million contract.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave. in Dorchester, say Boston police.
Boston police describe LaGrant-Gomes as a Black male, 5'8", with brown eyes and black hair, and seek public help.
Anyone with information on LaGrant-Gomes can contact District B-3 detectives or submit tips via CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, was arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
The female victim, 42, suffered life-threatening injuries, while the male victim, also 42, had non-life-threatening injuries.
Broomes faces multiple charges including assault with intent to murder; a dangerousness hearing is scheduled for May 21.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: A theoretical 2026 lineup of former Red Sox includes Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber, among others.
Five former Red Sox starting pitchers have a combined 2.74 ERA in 2026, outperforming current Boston starters.
Kyle Schwarber leads MLB in home runs with 18, while Dominic Smith surprises with a .353 batting average.
